mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate and supports equity multiples, so a downward FEDFUNDS trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts coincide with persistently negative forward equity performance.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window and the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 55% of such cases, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A high policy rate above a restrictive threshold raises real financing costs and compresses risk appetite, so S&P 500 forward returns are negative when FEDFUNDS is elevated. The effect should be strongest when inflation remains above trend, because real rates are then not falling fast enough to offset tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the sign reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation is above its prior 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and demand, which should precede negative equity returns over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if unemployment spikes do not coincide with weaker S&P 500 performance.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months and the next 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 55% of events, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales growth indicates improving final demand and revenue momentum, which should lift equity prices over the next few weeks. The signal is invalidated if sales growth accelerations fail to translate into positive forward market returns.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month growth in RSAFS is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A stronger industrial production trend signals improving output and capacity utilization, which should support cyclicals and the broad index over a one-quarter horizon. The hypothesis fails when industrial acceleration no longer precedes stronger equity performance.
failure_condition: If positive 3-month changes in INDPRO are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the signal’s Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 4-year rolling window, reject it.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts break above a growth threshold, the construction cycle and household wealth effects improve, supporting equity returns over the following month. This mechanism is falsified if housing surges do not correspond to positive market returns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST increases by more than 8% over 3 months and the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 55% of such cases.

mechanism_narrative: A stable labor market with rising payrolls and falling unemployment reflects sustained income growth and lower recession risk, which should produce positive equity returns over the next month. The signal requires both proxies because payroll gains alone are weaker when unemployment is also rising.
failure_condition: Reject if months with PAYEMS rising and UNRATE falling do not yield positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases, or if either proxy alone explains the sign better than the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-softening inflation regime, slowing CPI inflation combined with steady retail sales improves real purchasing power and lowers pressure for additional tightening, which should support equities over a one-month horizon. The mechanism should fail when disinflation is accompanied by collapsing demand.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with inflation below 2% annualized and non-declining RSAFS do not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the relation flips during periods of weakening sales.
